steps.title: Identify the requirement for a valuable proxy
steps.content: The text proposes that D. melanogaster could be a “valuable proxy” for X. laevis in testing Ag-NP bioaccumulation. A proxy is valuable only if it reliably predicts the behavior of the species it replaces.

steps.title: Apply the proxy requirement to the choices
steps.content:
The correct response directly shows that both species display comparable rates of Ag-NP uptake in comparable environments—precisely what the proxy hypothesis requires.

The other choices indicate the species respond differently or inconsistently to Ag-NPs, undermining the proxy relationship; an incorrect response discusses detection difficulty rather than whether bioaccumulation patterns align.

That confirms C is correct.
